title: A Casa e o Mobiliário
subtitle: The House, Furniture & SER/ESTAR/FICAR
description: Learn house vocabulary, rooms, furniture, the verb HAVER, and the distinction between SER, ESTAR, and FICAR for location.

vocabulary.example: O João mora num apartamento no Bairro Alto.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: João lives in an apartment in Bairro Alto.

vocabulary.word: a sala (de estar) / a cozinha / o quarto
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah SAH-lah / ah koh-ZEEN-yah / oo KWAHR-too
vocabulary.example: A casa tem uma sala, uma cozinha e dois quartos.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The house has a living room, a kitchen, and two bedrooms.
vocabulary.tip: Rooms: sala (living room), cozinha (kitchen), quarto (bedroom), casa de banho (bathroom)

vocabulary.word: a casa de banho / o corredor / a despensa
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah KAH-zah deh BAHN-yoo / oo koh-reh-DOR / ah deh-SHEN-sah
vocabulary.example: A casa de banho fica ao lado do quarto.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The bathroom is next to the bedroom.
vocabulary.tip: 'Casa de banho' = bathroom (literally 'house of bath'). 'WC' is also used in Portugal

vocabulary.word: a cama / o roupeiro / a mesa
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah KAH-mah / oo roh-PAY-roo / ah MEH-zah
vocabulary.example: No quarto há uma cama e um roupeiro.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the bedroom there is a bed and a wardrobe.
vocabulary.tip: Furniture is usually preceded by 'um/uma' when describing what's in a room

vocabulary.word: a banheira / o lavatório / a sanita
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah bahn-YAY-rah / oo lah-vah-TOH-ree-oo / ah sah-NEE-tah
vocabulary.example: A casa de banho tem uma banheira e um lavatório.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The bathroom has a bathtub and a sink.
vocabulary.tip: Essential bathroom vocabulary for describing your home

vocabulary.word: o fogão / o frigorífico / o forno
vocabulary.pronunciation: oo foh-GOWN / oo free-goh-REE-foo / oo FOR-noo
vocabulary.example: Na cozinha há um fogão, um frigorífico e um forno.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the kitchen there is a stove, a refrigerator, and an oven.
vocabulary.tip: Kitchen essentials: fogão (stove), frigorífico (fridge), forno (oven), lava-loiças (dishwasher)

vocabulary.word: o sofá / a televisão / o tapete
vocabulary.pronunciation: oo soh-FAH / ah teh-leh-vee-ZOWN / oo tah-PEH-teh
vocabulary.example: Na sala há um sofá, uma televisão e um tapete.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the living room there is a sofa, a television, and a rug.
vocabulary.tip: Living room items: sofá (sofa), televisão (TV), tapete (rug), quadro (painting)

vocabulary.translation: There is a computer in the living room.
vocabulary.example: Há um computador na sala. / A sala tem um computador.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: There is a computer in the living room. / The living room has a computer.
vocabulary.tip: HAVER (there is/are) is impersonal — always use 'há' (3rd person singular)

vocabulary.word: A casa é / fica no centro da cidade.
vocabulary.translation: The house is / is located in the city center.
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah KAH-zah eh / FEE-kah noh SEN-troo dah see-DAH-deh
vocabulary.ipa: /ɐ ˈkazɐ ˈɛ/ / /ˈfikɐ nu ˈsɛtɾu dɐ siˈdadɨ/
vocabulary.example: A casa do João é/fica no centro da cidade.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: João's house is in the city center.
vocabulary.tip: SER and FICAR are interchangeable for permanent location of buildings. ESTAR is for people/objects.

vocabulary.word: O quarto fica entre a sala e a casa de banho.
vocabulary.translation: The bedroom is between the living room and the bathroom.
vocabulary.pronunciation: oo KWAHR-too FEE-kah EN-treh ah SAH-lah ee ah KAH-zah deh BAHN-yoo
vocabulary.ipa: /u ˈkwartu ˈfikɐ ˈẽtɾɨ ɐ ˈsalɐ i ɐ ˈkazɐ dɨ ˈbɐɲu/
vocabulary.example: A cozinha fica entre a sala e a despensa.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The kitchen is between the living room and the pantry.
vocabulary.tip: Use FICAR or É for describing where rooms are located within a house

sections.heading: Types of Houses & Rooms
sections.body: Portuguese has several words for types of dwellings: 'apartamento' (apartment/flat), 'moradia' (detached house), 'vivenda' (villa), 'andar' (floor/storey). A typical house has these rooms: 'sala' (living room), 'cozinha' (kitchen), 'quarto' (bedroom), 'casa de banho' (bathroom), 'corredor' (hallway), 'despensa' (pantry), 'jardim' (garden), 'varanda' (balcony). When describing your home, use TER for what it has: 'A casa tem dois quartos' (The house has two bedrooms). Use FICAR or É for where it is: 'A casa fica no centro' (The house is in the center).

sections.heading: Furniture by Room
sections.body: Each room has characteristic furniture. Bedroom: cama (bed), roupeiro (wardrobe), mesa de cabeceira (nightstand). Kitchen: fogão (stove), frigorífico (refrigerator), forno (oven), lava-loiças (dishwasher). Living room: sofá (sofa), televisão (TV), tapete (rug), quadro (painting). Bathroom: banheira (bathtub), lavatório (sink), sanita (toilet), duche (shower). When describing what's in a room, use HAVER: 'No quarto há uma cama' (In the bedroom there is a bed).

sections.heading: SER vs ESTAR vs FICAR — Three Verbs for Location
sections.body: Portuguese has three verbs that relate to location: SER, ESTAR, and FICAR. SER and FICAR are used for permanent locations of buildings, cities, and spaces: 'A casa é/fica no centro' (The house is in the center). ESTAR is used for temporary locations of people, animals, and movable objects: 'O João está em casa' (João is at home). FICAR adds a nuance of 'being located' or 'being situated' — it's very common when giving addresses or describing where places are. In many cases, SER and FICAR are interchangeable for location: 'O banco é/fica ao lado da farmácia'.

sections.heading: SER vs ESTAR vs FICAR — Conjugation

items.portuguese: A casa do João é/fica no Bairro Alto. O João está em Lisboa.
items.translation: João's house is in Bairro Alto. João is in Lisbon.
items.pronunciation: ah KAH-zah doo ZHOH-ow eh/FEE-kah noh BAHR-yoo AHL-too. oo ZHOH-ow esh-TAH eem LEESH-boh-ah.

items.portuguese: O Marquês de Pombal é/fica no centro de Lisboa.
items.translation: The Marquês de Pombal is in the center of Lisbon.
items.pronunciation: oo mar-KEH deh Pom-BAHL eh/FEE-kah noh SEN-troo deh LEESH-boh-ah.

items.portuguese: A carteira do António está em cima da mesa.
items.translation: António's wallet is on the table.
items.pronunciation: ah kahr-TAY-rah doh ahn-TOH-nee-oo esh-TAH ah-SEE-mah dah MEH-zah.

sections.heading: The Verb HAVER — There Is / There Are
sections.body: The verb HAVER (to have / there is / there are) is used to indicate existence. When meaning 'there is/are', it is IMPERSONAL — it only exists in the 3rd person singular: 'há'. It doesn't matter if the noun is singular or plural: 'Há um computador' (There is a computer) / 'Há alunos na sala' (There are students in the room). HAVER can replace TER when introduced with the preposition 'em': 'O jardim tem muitas flores' = 'No jardim há muitas flores' (In the garden there are many flowers). In negative sentences, use 'não há' (there isn't/aren't).

sections.heading: HAVER vs TER for Existence
rows: There is a shop in Paris.
In Paris there is a shop.
There are many casinos in Macau.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes & Tips
items: SER and FICAR are interchangeable for permanent location: 'A casa é/fica no centro'. Both are correct.
ESTAR is for temporary location of people/objects: 'Estou em casa' (I'm at home), NOT 'Sou em casa'.
HAVER is always 'há' for 'there is/are' — never use 'hão' or 'havem'. It's impersonal.
Don't confuse 'há' (there is) with 'à' (at the). 'Há' comes from HAVER; 'à' is a preposition contraction.
When describing rooms, use FICAR for location within the house: 'O quarto fica ao lado da cozinha'.
For furniture, match the item to the room: 'cama' goes in 'quarto', 'fogão' goes in 'cozinha'.
The verb HAVER can replace TER: 'A cidade tem um templo' = 'Na cidade há um templo'.

exercises.title: SER, ESTAR, or FICAR?
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct verb for each sentence.

items.question: A biblioteca ___ no centro da cidade.
items.explanation: Use SER for permanent location of buildings. 'A biblioteca é no centro' = The library is in the center.

items.question: A caneta azul ___ na mala.
items.explanation: Use ESTAR for temporary location of objects. 'A caneta está na mala' = The pen is in the bag.

items.question: Nós ___ na sala de aula.
items.explanation: Use ESTAR for location of people. 'Estamos na sala de aula' = We are in the classroom.

items.question: Portugal ___ no sul da Europa.
items.explanation: Use ESTAR for location of countries/regions. 'Portugal está no sul da Europa' = Portugal is in southern Europe.

exercises.title: Complete with HAVER or TER
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank with the correct form of HAVER or TER.

items.sentence: Em Paris, ___ uma loja de produtos portugueses.
Paris ___ uma loja de produtos portugueses.
A cidade de Évora ___ o templo de Diana.
Em Évora, ___ o templo de Diana.

exercises.title: Match the Furniture to the Room
exercises.instructions: Select the item that does NOT belong in each room.

items.question: Which item does NOT belong in the kitchen?
items.explanation: 'Duche' (shower) belongs in the bathroom, not the kitchen.

items.question: Which item does NOT belong in the bedroom?
items.explanation: 'Frigorífico' (fridge) belongs in the kitchen, not the bedroom.

items.question: Which item does NOT belong in the bathroom?
items.explanation: 'Sofá' (sofa) belongs in the living room, not the bathroom.

exercises.title: Describe a House
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence about a house.

items.sentence: A casa ___ no centro da cidade. (is located)
No quarto ___ uma cama e um roupeiro. (there is)
A cozinha ___ entre a sala e a despensa. (is)
items.hint: FICAR for location of rooms within a house
